name: Ford Dabney
abstract: Ford Thompson Dabney (15 March 1883 – 6 June 1958) was an American ragtime pianist, composer, songwriter, and acclaimed director of bands and orchestras for Broadway musical theater, revues, vaudeville, and early recordings. Additionally, for two years in Washington, from 1910 to 1912, he was proprietor of a theater that featured vaudevi…

about_quotes.speaker: Dominic James "Nick" La Rocca
about_quotes.evidence: there was Ford Dabney on a number of different labels... But one's only got to listen to those... to realise that the one is Ragtime and the other is Jazz.

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: I never saw him... I think I saw him once. He always lived high-class, up on Sugar Hill. And Ford Dabney was a big-time player for these millionaires.

about_quotes.speaker: Quentin Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: There was another one, too, called Ford Dabney. ... He was just a -- he never played nothing but society gigs and society parties.

about_quotes.speaker: Jacques Butler
about_quotes.evidence: I heard this Ike Dixon and Ford Dabney then Fletcher Henderson come down and shook everybody up

about_quotes.speaker: Monk Montgomery
about_quotes.evidence: The guys like Ford Dabney and Jim Europe, they had all the gig work.
